question: Remove the full-MAXC KV-copy tax (~1.4ms/tok, MEASURED transfer +1.5ms/+8tok/s) without violating tinygrad's pure TinyJit graph model.

A_two_graph_append_then_attention.what_changes: realize the per-layer KV append as its own graph BEFORE the attention graph (gpt2 .assign().realize() pattern), so the attention reads the already-updated persistent cache
A_two_graph_append_then_attention.why_might_work: separate graphs avoid the SAME-graph read-after-write reduce hazard (the hazard is intra-graph)
A_two_graph_append_then_attention.blocker_risk: breaks the single fused @function(precompile=True) _run per layer -> the append must move OUTSIDE _run; 36 separate append realizes/token add launch+ordering overhead; ordering across graphs without HOST sync is the open risk
A_two_graph_append_then_attention.first_gate: two-graph append+read microbench: no full-MAXC copy + append-graph overhead <0.25ms/tok + device-side ordering (no host sync)

B_runtime_kv_object_pointer_length.what_changes: a runtime object owns the persistent cache; the opaque append (proven, extra/qk_kv_cache_state_token.py) writes it, and an OPAQUE attention node reads it via pointer+length -- both bypass functional .after()/copy
B_runtime_kv_object_pointer_length.why_might_work: two opaque custom_kernels (write then read same buffer) give a clean buffer dependency, NOT a tinygrad REDUCE-over-mutated-buffer hazard
B_runtime_kv_object_pointer_length.blocker_risk: the DEFAULT gqa_coop_vec attention is a functional tinygrad reduce -> would re-hit the hazard; so this COUPLES to an opaque attention read (the B4 owned tile already reads cache_kv natively). Lifecycle: reset/batch/prompt-boundary/server concurrency must be managed by the runtime object.
B_runtime_kv_object_pointer_length.first_gate: opaque append + owned-tile-reads-persistent-cache (no assigned_kv copy), byte-identical 64-tok, W==D >=+5%@1024

C_state_token_hcq_only.what_changes: inject an HCQ-graph-level dependency token to order append before attention without functional .after()
C_state_token_hcq_only.why_might_work: HCQ ordering is below the functional scheduler
C_state_token_hcq_only.blocker_risk: the model graph is built functionally THEN lowered to HCQ; the attention READ is still a functional reduce that the scheduler materializes (copy) before HCQ -> the token doesn't remove the copy. Reduces to B (need opaque read).

D_alias_aware_same_graph_mutation.what_changes: a scheduler rule allowing in-place slice-assign + same-graph prefix read
D_alias_aware_same_graph_mutation.why_might_work: would let the default attention read the mutated buffer
D_alias_aware_same_graph_mutation.blocker_risk: needs symbolic-RANGE alias analysis under symbolic start_pos -> hits the _eval/_min_max symbolic wall + symbolic-size buffer reject; UNBOUNDED (prior task KV_ALIAS_RULE_UNBOUNDED)

E_paged_block_table.blocker_risk: requires page-table-aware attention kernels = reopens attention layout (out of scope); large

correctness_hazards: stale KV across prompts (runtime object must reset)
JIT replay with changing start_pos (proven OK for the opaque append)
batch>1 (kernel is B==1 only; needs generalization)
prefill->decode handoff (concrete prefill vs symbolic decode)
speculative/multi-token decode (T>1; append kernel is T==1)

files: a new opaque/pointer-length attention read OR route through extra/qk_owned_flash_decode_graph_node.py
blast_radius.core_tinygrad_change: NO if using opaque custom_kernels (write+read); YES only for design D (alias rule)
blast_radius.upstreamable: the runtime-KV-object pattern is fork-local; a clean version could be upstreamed

verdict_reason: copy removal is MEASURED-transferable (~1.4ms/+8tok/s) and needs NO core tinygrad change, BUT the default functional gqa_coop_vec attention reduce over the cache re-hits the read-after-write hazard; eliminating the copy REQUIRES the attention to read the persistent cache via an OPAQUE/pointer-length node (the owned tile) -> it couples to Lane 2's owned-attention route. Design B (runtime KV object + owned-tile read) is the bounded-ish path; two-graph (A) is feasible but restructures the fused forward.
